content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges [(0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), (0, 2, 2), (5, 8, 3), (0, 8, 4), (6, 9, 4), (1, 4, 6), (1, 8, 10), (5, 7, 11), (4, 8, 12), (0, 7, 16), (2, 8, 18), (0, 4, 23), (6, 7, 25), (1, 5, 26)], we need to find when node 5 and node 8 are first connected.

We will scan each edge and collect the time t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

First, we have (0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), and (0, 2, 2). We don't have node 5 and node 8 yet, so we move to the next edge.

Next, we have (5, 8, 3) and (0, 8, 4). We don't have node 0 and node 8 yet, so we move to the next edge.

Then, we have (6, 9, 4) and (1, 4, 6). We don't have node 6 and node 9 yet, so we move to the next edge.

After that, we have (1, 8, 10) and (5, 7, 11). We don't have node 1 and node 7 yet, so we move to the next edge.

Finally, we have (4, 8, 12) and (0, 7, 16). We don't have node 4 and node 7 yet, so we move to the next edge.

We have (2, 8, 18) and (0, 4, 23). We don't have node 2 and node 4 yet, so we move to the next edge.

Lastly, we have (6, 7, 25) and (1, 5, 26). We don't have node 6 and node 5 yet, so we move to the next edge.

Now we have all the necessary edges. We can see that node 5 and node 8 are first connected at time 16.

Answer: 16
